title: GIF Image
documentation:
## Purpose
Represents a GIF image, which may be a static raster image or an animated sequence of frames. GIF supports a maximum palette of 256 colours per frame and lossless compression.

## When to use
Use `media:gif;image` when a node produces or consumes GIF files, particularly animated GIFs. For static images requiring more colours or better compression, prefer [PNG Image](https://capdag.com/browse/media:png) or [WebP Image](https://capdag.com/browse/media:webp;image).

## Notes
GIF supports transparency via a single transparent colour index, not an alpha channel. Animated GIFs can have large file sizes; consumers that display or process animations should be prepared for multi-frame content. Modern alternatives such as WebP or AVIF offer better compression for equivalent quality.
